198	def _resolve_draft_vocab_size(source: str) -> int:
199	    """Load ``draft_vocab_size`` from one existing local JSON file."""
200	
201	    expanded = Path(source).expanduser()
202	    if not expanded.is_file():
203	        raise FileNotFoundError(
204	            "--draft-model-config must point to an existing local JSON file: "
205	            f"{source}"
206	        )
207	    if expanded.suffix.lower() != ".json":
208	        raise ValueError(
209	            f"--draft-model-config must point to a local .json file: {source}"
210	        )
211	    try:
212	        with expanded.open(encoding="utf-8") as stream:
213	            payload = json.load(stream)
214	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
215	        raise ValueError(f"invalid draft config JSON {expanded}: {exc}") from exc
216	    if not isinstance(payload, dict):
217	        raise ValueError(f"draft config JSON {expanded} must contain an object")
218	    value = payload.get("draft_vocab_size", payload.get("vocab_size"))
219	
220	    if isinstance(value, bool) or not isinstance(value, int) or value <= 0:
221	        raise ValueError(
222	            f"draft model config {source!r} must define a positive "
223	            f"draft_vocab_size (or vocab_size fallback), got {value!r}"
224	        )
225	    return value

228	def _generate_shared_vocab_mapping(
229	    dataset,
230	    *,
231	    output_path: str,
232	    target_vocab_size: int,
233	    draft_vocab_size: int,
234	) -> str:
235	    """Generate one mapping on global rank 0 and propagate failures to peers."""
236	
237	    mapping_dir = os.path.join(output_path, "vocab_mapping")
238	    mapping_path = os.path.join(mapping_dir, "vocab_mapping.pt")
239	    result = [None]
240	    if dist.get_rank() == 0:
241	        temporary_path = None
242	        try:
243	            temporary_key = f".vocab_mapping.{uuid.uuid4().hex}"
244	            temporary_path = generate_vocab_mapping_file(
245	                dataset=dataset,
246	                target_vocab_size=target_vocab_size,
247	                draft_vocab_size=draft_vocab_size,
248	                cache_dir=mapping_dir,
249	                cache_key=temporary_key,
250	            )
251	            mapping = torch.load(
252	                temporary_path,
253	                map_location="cpu",
254	                weights_only=True,
255	            )
256	            d2t = mapping.get("d2t")
257	            t2d = mapping.get("t2d")
258	            if not isinstance(d2t, torch.Tensor) or tuple(d2t.shape) != (
259	                draft_vocab_size,
260	            ):
261	                raise ValueError(
262	                    f"{temporary_path} has invalid d2t shape; expected "
263	                    f"({draft_vocab_size},), got {getattr(d2t, 'shape', None)}"
264	                )
265	            if not isinstance(t2d, torch.Tensor) or tuple(t2d.shape) != (
266	                target_vocab_size,
267	            ):
268	                raise ValueError(
269	                    f"{temporary_path} has invalid t2d shape; expected "
270	                    f"({target_vocab_size},), got {getattr(t2d, 'shape', None)}"
271	                )
272	            os.replace(temporary_path, mapping_path)
273	            temporary_path = None
274	            result[0] = {"path": mapping_path}
275	        except BaseException as exc:
276	            result[0] = {
277	                "error": f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}",
278	            }
279	        finally:
280	            if temporary_path is not None:
281	                try:
282	                    os.remove(temporary_path)
283	                except OSError:
284	                    pass
285	
286	    dist.broadcast_object_list(result, src=0)
287	    if result[0] is None or "error" in result[0]:
288	        reason = "rank 0 did not publish a result"
289	        if result[0] is not None:
290	            reason = result[0]["error"]
291	        raise RuntimeError(f"failed to generate vocabulary mapping: {reason}")
292	    if result[0]["path"] != mapping_path:
293	        raise RuntimeError(
294	            "vocabulary mapping generator returned an unexpected path: "
295	            f"{result[0]['path']} != {mapping_path}"
296	        )
297	    return mapping_path

316	def resolve_offline_capture_plan(
317	    args: argparse.Namespace,
318	    target_config: AutoConfig,
319	) -> OfflineCapturePlan:
320	    """Resolve capture layers and output keys through the algorithm registry."""
321	
322	    strategy = getattr(args, "strategy", "eagle3")
323	    model = {
324	        "target_model_path": args.target_model_path,
325	        "draft_model_config": getattr(args, "draft_model_config", None),
326	        "trust_remote_code": args.trust_remote_code,
327	        "cache_dir": getattr(args, "cache_dir", None),
328	    }
329	    cfg = Config(
330	        model=model,
331	        data={
332	            "hidden_states_path": args.output_path or "__offline_capture__",
333	            "max_length": args.max_length,
334	        },
335	        training={"strategy": strategy},
336	    )
337	    resolved = resolve_offline_capture(cfg, target_config=target_config)
338	    return OfflineCapturePlan(
339	        strategy=resolved.run.algorithm.name,
340	        draft_config=resolved.draft_config,
341	        capture_method=resolved.capture_method,
342	        capture_layers=resolved.capture_layers,
343	        layout=resolved.layout,
344	    )

347	def build_target_model(
348	    args: argparse.Namespace,
349	    model_config: AutoConfig,
350	    capture_layers: List[int],
351	    capture_method: str = "eagle3",
352	) -> OfflineSGLangCapture:
353	    """Build the local target used only by this preprocessing command."""
354	    target_model = load_offline_capture(
355	        args.target_model_path,
356	        torch_dtype=(
357	            model_config.dtype
358	            if hasattr(model_config, "dtype")
359	            else model_config.torch_dtype
360	        ),
361	        trust_remote_code=args.trust_remote_code,
362	        **_sglang_kwargs(args),
363	    )
364	    target_model.set_capture_layers(
365	        capture_layers,
366	        capture_method=capture_method,
367	    )
368	    return target_model
369	
370	
371	class HiddenStatesGenerator:
372	    """
373	    This is a generator for creating and saving the hidden states based on the target model.
374	    It includes the following features:
375	        1. Fixes a potential deadlock in TP > 1 scenarios when a batch is skipped.
376	        2. Implements a context manager (`with` statement) for robust resource handling.
377	        3. Makes internal settings (like queue sizes, group sizes) configurable.
378	        4. Centralizes resource cleanup logic.
379	    """
380	
381	    def __init__(
382	        self,
383	        target_model,
384	        capture_layout: Optional[OfflineCaptureLayout] = None,
385	        num_io_threads: int = 4,
386	        io_queue_size: int = 50,
387	        file_group_size: int = 2000,
388	        compress: bool = False,
389	        compression_level: int = 6,
390	    ):
391	        """
392	        Args:
393	            target_model: The model for inference.
394	            capture_layout: Algorithm-owned mapping from captured states to files.
395	            num_io_threads: Number of threads for async I/O.
396	            io_queue_size: Max number of pending I/O futures before cleanup.
397	            file_group_size: Number of files per subdirectory.
398	        """
399	        self.model = target_model
400	        self.capture_layout = capture_layout or OfflineCaptureLayout(
401	            capture_method="eagle3",
402	            aux_feature="aux_hidden_state",
403	            last_hidden_feature="hidden_state",
404	            passthrough=(
405	                ("input_ids", "input_ids"),
406	                ("loss_mask", "loss_mask"),
407	            ),
408	        )
409	
410	        # --- Configurable parameters ---
411	        self.num_io_threads = num_io_threads
412	        self.io_queue_size = io_queue_size
413	        self.file_group_size = file_group_size
414	        self.compress = compress
415	        self.compression_level = compression_level
416	        self.file_extension = ".ckpt.gz" if self.compress else ".ckpt"
417	
418	        # progress bar should only shown on TP rank = 0
419	        self.show_progress = dist.get_rank(get_tp_group()) == 0
420	
421	        # --- REFACTOR: Thread pool is now managed by __enter__ and __exit__ ---
422	        self.io_executor = None
423	        self.pending_futures = []
424	
425	    def __enter__(self):
426	        """Initializes resources when entering a 'with' block."""
427	        if is_tp_rank_0():
428	            self.io_executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=self.num_io_threads)
429	        self.pending_futures = []
430	        return self
431	
432	    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb):
433	        """Cleans up resources when exiting a 'with' block."""
434	        if is_tp_rank_0() and self.io_executor is not None:
435	            if self.show_progress:
436	                print("\nWaiting for all async I/O operations to complete...")
437	            self._wait_all_saves()
438	            self.io_executor.shutdown(wait=True)
439	            self.io_executor = None  # Reset for safety
440	
441	        # Final barrier to ensure all processes exit generate() cleanly
442	        dist.barrier()
443	
444	    def _save_tensor_sync(
445	        self,
446	        record: Mapping[str, torch.Tensor],
447	        output_file: str,
448	    ) -> None:
449	        """
450	        Save a feature record synchronously, skipping records containing NaNs.
451	
452	        Args:
453	            record: The algorithm-specific feature record to save.
454	            output_file (str): The path to the output file.
455	        """
456	        persisted = dict(record)
457	        for feature_name, tensor in persisted.items():
458	            if not torch.is_tensor(tensor):
459	                raise TypeError(
460	                    f"offline feature {feature_name!r} must be a tensor, got "
461	                    f"{type(tensor).__name__}"
462	                )
463	            if (torch.is_floating_point(tensor) or torch.is_complex(tensor)) and bool(
464	                torch.isnan(tensor).any()
465	            ):
466	                print(
467	                    f"Warning: NaN found in {feature_name} for {output_file}. "
468	                    "Skipping save."
469	                )
470	                return
471	
472	        if self.compress:
473	            with gzip.open(
474	                output_file, "wb", compresslevel=self.compression_level
475	            ) as f:
476	                torch.save(persisted, f)
477	        else:
478	            torch.save(persisted, output_file)
479	
480	    def _save_tensor_async(
481	        self,
482	        record: Mapping[str, torch.Tensor],
483	        output_file: str,
484	    ) -> None:
485	        """
486	        Submit a job to the io_executor to save the data point asynchronously.
487	
488	        Args:
489	            record: The algorithm-specific feature record to save.
490	            output_file (str): The path to the output file.
491	        """
492	        assert is_tp_rank_0(), "Only tp_rank=0 should call _save_tensor_async"
493	        # If the queue of pending save operations is full, we must wait.
494	        if len(self.pending_futures) >= self.io_queue_size:
495	            # First, try to clear any futures that have already finished without waiting.
496	            self.pending_futures = [f for f in self.pending_futures if not f.done()]
497	            # If the queue is *still* full, it means all I/O threads are busy and we have
498	            # a backlog. We must now block the main generation loop and wait for the
499	            # oldest I/O operation to complete before proceeding.
500	            if len(self.pending_futures) >= self.io_queue_size:
501	                self.pending_futures.pop(0).result()
502	
503	        future = self.io_executor.submit(self._save_tensor_sync, record, output_file)
504	        self.pending_futures.append(future)
505	
506	    def _wait_all_saves(self):
507	        """
508	        This method is to ensure that all submitted jobs are completed.
509	        """
510	        if is_tp_rank_0() and self.pending_futures:
511	            for future in tqdm(
512	                self.pending_futures,
513	                desc="Finalizing Writes",
514	                disable=not self.show_progress,
515	            ):
516	                future.result()  # Wait and raise exception if any
517	            self.pending_futures.clear()
518	
519	    def _prepare_output_dirs(
520	        self, output_path: str, start_idx: int, total_samples: int
521	    ) -> None:
522	        """
523	        The dataset is organized into groups of files, each group has a folder which contains the files for this group. For example, if the
524	        file_group_size is 2000, the 0-1999 samples will be saved in the folder "rows_0-2000", the 2000-3999 samples will be saved in the folder "rows_2000-4000", etc.
525	
526	        Args:
527	            output_path (str): The path to the output directory.
528	            start_idx (int): The starting index of the samples to save.
529	            total_samples (int): The total number of samples to save.
530	
531	        Returns:
532	            None
533	        """
534	        if not is_tp_rank_0() or total_samples == 0:
535	            return
536	        start_group = (start_idx // self.file_group_size) * self.file_group_size
537	        end_sample_idx = start_idx + total_samples - 1
538	        end_group = (end_sample_idx // self.file_group_size) * self.file_group_size
539	        for group_start_idx in range(start_group, end_group + 1, self.file_group_size):
540	            grouped_subdir = (
541	                f"rows_{group_start_idx}-{group_start_idx + self.file_group_size}"
542	            )
543	            output_dir = os.path.join(output_path, grouped_subdir)
544	            os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)
545	
546	    def _check_existing_files_batch(
547	        self, output_path: str, global_indices: List[int]
548	    ) -> List[bool]:
549	        """
550	        A helper function to check if the files for the given global indices exist.
551	
552	        Args:
553	            output_path (str): The path to the output directory.
554	            global_indices (List[int]): The global indices of the samples to check.
555	
556	        Returns:
557	            List[bool]: A list of booleans indicating if the files for the given global indices exist.
558	        """
559	        if not is_tp_rank_0():
560	            return [False] * len(global_indices)
561	
562	        def check_single_file(idx):
563	            if os.path.exists(self._get_file_path(output_path, idx)):
564	                return True
565	            uncompressed_ckpt = self._get_file_path(output_path, idx, extension=".ckpt")
566	            compressed_ckpt = self._get_file_path(
567	                output_path, idx, extension=".ckpt.gz"
568	            )
569	            return os.path.exists(uncompressed_ckpt) or os.path.exists(compressed_ckpt)
570	
571	        # Parallel file existence check
572	        with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=self.num_io_threads) as executor:
573	            exists = list(executor.map(check_single_file, global_indices))
574	        return exists
575	
576	    def _get_file_path(
577	        self, output_path: str, idx: int, extension: Optional[str] = None
578	    ) -> str:
579	        """
580	        A helper function to get the standard file path for the data point with the given index.
581	
582	        Args:
583	            output_path (str): The path to the output directory.
584	            idx (int): The global index of the data point.
585	
586	        Returns:
587	            str: The file path for the data point.
588	        """
589	        ext = self.file_extension if extension is None else extension
590	        group_idx = (idx // self.file_group_size) * self.file_group_size
591	        grouped_subdir = f"rows_{group_idx}-{group_idx + self.file_group_size}"
592	        return os.path.join(output_path, grouped_subdir, f"data_{idx}{ext}")
593	
594	    @torch.no_grad()
595	    def generate(
596	        self,
597	        data_loader: torch.utils.data.DataLoader,
598	        output_path: str,
599	        start_idx: int = 0,
600	        samples_per_dp: int = 0,
601	    ):
602	        """
603	        This version prioritizes minimal CPU RAM usage above all else, even at the cost of performance.
604	        - It processes samples one-by-one within the tp_rank_0 process.
605	        - It avoids batching GPU-to-CPU transfers.
606	        - It ensures only one sample's data is in RAM for I/O at any given time.
607	        """
608	        self._prepare_output_dirs(output_path, start_idx, samples_per_dp)
609	
610	        tp_group = get_tp_group()
611	        tp_group_ranks = dist.get_process_group_ranks(tp_group)
612	        tp_rank_0_global = tp_group_ranks[0]
613	        global_idx = start_idx
614	
615	        progress_bar = tqdm(
616	            data_loader,
617	            disable=(not self.show_progress),
618	            desc="Generating Hidden States",
619	            position=dist.get_rank(get_dp_group()),
620	            leave=True,
621	        )
622	
623	        total_skipped, total_processed = 0, 0
624	
625	        for batch_idx, batch in enumerate(progress_bar):
626	            batch_size = batch["input_ids"].size(0)
627	            current_batch_indices = list(range(global_idx, global_idx + batch_size))
628	
629	            # # Step 1: Synchronize valid indices across TP group
630	            # we check which files already exist and sync this info across TP ranks
631	            # if exists, we will skip these samples
632	            if is_tp_rank_0():
633	                exists_list = self._check_existing_files_batch(
634	                    output_path, current_batch_indices
635	                )
636	                exists_tensor = torch.tensor(
637	                    exists_list, dtype=torch.bool, device="cuda"
638	                )
639	            else:
640	                exists_tensor = torch.tensor(
641	                    [False] * batch_size, dtype=torch.bool, device="cuda"
642	                )
643	            dist.broadcast(exists_tensor, src=tp_rank_0_global, group=tp_group)
644	
645	            # Step 1: TP rank 0 checks which samples need processing
646	            valid_indices_in_batch = [
647	                i for i, exists in enumerate(exists_tensor) if not exists
648	            ]
649	            sample_global_indices = [
650	                current_batch_indices[i] for i in valid_indices_in_batch
651	            ]
652	            num_valid = len(valid_indices_in_batch)
653	            total_skipped += batch_size - num_valid
654	
655	            # Step 2: Filter batch before moving to GPU to save memory
656	            global_idx += batch_size
657	            filtered_batch = {
658	                "input_ids": batch["input_ids"][valid_indices_in_batch],
659	                "attention_mask": batch["attention_mask"][valid_indices_in_batch],
660	                "loss_mask": batch["loss_mask"][valid_indices_in_batch],
661	            }
662	            del batch
663	            if num_valid == 0:
664	                # Data has already been generated, no sample processing, update progress bar.
665	                if self.show_progress:
666	                    progress_bar.set_postfix(
667	                        {
668	                            "processed": total_processed,
669	                            "skipped": total_skipped,
670	                            "pending_io": (
671	                                len(self.pending_futures) if is_tp_rank_0() else 0
672	                            ),
673	                        }
674	                    )
675	                continue
676	
677	            filtered_batch_gpu = {
678	                k: v.cuda(non_blocking=True) for k, v in filtered_batch.items()
679	            }
680	            captured = self.model.capture(
681	                **filtered_batch_gpu,
682	            )
683	            aux_hidden_states_list = captured.hidden_states
684	            last_hidden_states_list = captured.last_hidden_states
685	            if aux_hidden_states_list is None:
686	                aux_hidden_states_list = [None] * num_valid
687	            if last_hidden_states_list is None:
688	                last_hidden_states_list = [None] * num_valid
689	
690	            del filtered_batch_gpu
691	
692	            if is_tp_rank_0():
693	                for i, (
694	                    current_global_idx,
695	                    aux_hidden_states,
696	                    last_hidden_states,
697	                ) in enumerate(
698	                    zip(
699	                        sample_global_indices,
700	                        aux_hidden_states_list,
701	                        last_hidden_states_list,
702	                    )
703	                ):
704	
705	                    # Process ONE sample at a time to minimize CPU RAM footprint
706	                    # 1. Transfer only the required slice for one sample to CPU
707	                    aux_hidden_states = (
708	                        aux_hidden_states.cpu().clone().unsqueeze(0)
709	                        if aux_hidden_states is not None
710	                        else None
711	                    )
712	                    last_hidden_states = (
713	                        last_hidden_states.cpu().clone().unsqueeze(0)
714	                        if last_hidden_states is not None
715	                        else None
716	                    )
717	                    record = self.capture_layout.materialize(
718	                        {
719	                            "input_ids": filtered_batch["input_ids"][i].clone(),
720	                            "loss_mask": filtered_batch["loss_mask"][i].clone(),
721	                            "aux_hidden_states": aux_hidden_states,
722	                            "last_hidden_states": last_hidden_states,
723	                        }
724	                    )
725	
726	                    # 3. Save asynchronously (the backpressure logic is still crucial)
727	                    output_file = self._get_file_path(output_path, current_global_idx)
728	                    self._save_tensor_async(record, output_file)
729	
730	                    # 4. Immediately clean up the single-sample CPU tensors
731	                    del last_hidden_states, aux_hidden_states
732	
733	                total_processed += len(sample_global_indices)
734	
735	            # Clean up the large GPU and CPU batch data
736	            del aux_hidden_states_list, last_hidden_states_list, filtered_batch
737	
738	            if batch_idx % 5 == 0:  # Make GC and cache clearing more frequent
739	                torch.cuda.empty_cache()
740	                gc.collect()
741	
742	            if self.show_progress:
743	                progress_bar.set_postfix(
744	                    {
745	                        "processed": total_processed,
746	                        "skipped": total_skipped,
747	                        "pending_io": (
748	                            len(self.pending_futures) if is_tp_rank_0() else 0
749	                        ),
750	                    }
751	                )
752	
753	        if self.show_progress:
754	            print(
755	                f"\nGeneration loop finished. Processed: {total_processed}, Skipped: {total_skipped}"
756	            )
757	        dist.barrier()
758	
759	
760	def main():
761	    args = parse_args()
762	    if args.num_io_threads is None:
763	        cpu_cores = os.cpu_count() or 1
764	        args.num_io_threads = max(1, cpu_cores)
765	    if args.output_path is None:
766	        args.output_path = os.path.join(
767	            Path(__file__).parent.parent, "cache", "hidden_states"
768	        )
769	
770	    target_model_config = AutoConfig.from_pretrained(
771	        args.target_model_path,
772	        cache_dir=args.cache_dir,
773	        trust_remote_code=args.trust_remote_code,
774	    )
775	    capture_plan = resolve_offline_capture_plan(args, target_model_config)
776	    draft_vocab_size = _resolve_draft_vocab_size(args.draft_model_config)
777	
778	    # Initialize distributed environment (TP + DP)
779	    init_distributed(timeout=args.dist_timeout, tp_size=args.tp_size)
780	    print_args_with_dots(args)
781	    if dist.get_rank() == 0:
782	        print("Resolved draft model config:")
783	        print(capture_plan.draft_config.to_json_string(use_diff=False))
784	    print_with_rank(
785	        f"Resolved {capture_plan.strategy} offline "
786	        f"capture method {capture_plan.capture_method!r}, layers: "
787	        f"{list(capture_plan.capture_layers)}"
788	    )
789	
790	    # Build target model (with TP)
791	    target_model = build_target_model(
792	        args,
793	        target_model_config,
794	        capture_layers=list(capture_plan.capture_layers),
795	        capture_method=capture_plan.capture_method,
796	    )
797	    target_text_config = getattr(
798	        target_model_config, "text_config", target_model_config
799	    )
800	    target_vocab_size = int(target_text_config.vocab_size)
801	    if draft_vocab_size > target_vocab_size:
802	        raise ValueError(
803	            f"draft_vocab_size={draft_vocab_size} exceeds target "
804	            f"vocab_size={target_vocab_size}"
805	        )
806	
807	    print_with_rank(
808	        f"DP Rank {dist.get_rank(get_dp_group())}, TP Rank {dist.get_rank(get_tp_group())}, "
809	        f"DP Size {dist.get_world_size(get_dp_group())}, TP Size {dist.get_world_size(get_tp_group())}"
810	    )
811	
812	    # Load complete dataset
813	    assert os.path.exists(
814	        args.data_path
815	    ), f"Dataset path {args.data_path} does not exist"
816	
817	    with rank_0_priority():
818	        print_with_rank("Loading/building dataset cache...")
819	        dataset = Dataset.from_generator(
820	            generator=safe_conversations_generator,
821	            gen_kwargs={"file_path": args.data_path},
822	            cache_dir=os.path.join(
823	                os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))),
824	                "cache",
825	                "hf_dataset",
826	            ),
827	            num_proc=min(args.build_dataset_num_proc, 32),
828	        )
829	    if args.num_samples is not None:
830	        dataset = dataset.select(range(args.num_samples))
831	    # Tokenizer and cache key
832	    tokenizer = load_tokenizer(
833	        args.target_model_path, trust_remote_code=args.trust_remote_code
834	    )
835	    cache_params_string = f"{args.data_path}-{args.max_length}-{args.chat_template}-{args.target_model_path}-{args.num_samples}-{args.is_preformatted}"
836	    cache_key = hashlib.md5(cache_params_string.encode()).hexdigest()
837	
838	    # Preprocess on complete, un-sharded dataset
839	    with rank_0_priority():
840	        print_with_rank("Main process is building the dataset cache...")
841	        eagle3_dataset = build_eagle3_dataset(
842	            dataset=dataset,
843	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
844	            chat_template=args.chat_template,
845	            max_length=args.max_length,
846	            cache_dir=os.path.join(args.cache_dir, "processed_dataset"),
847	            cache_key=cache_key,
848	            is_preformatted=args.is_preformatted,
849	            num_proc=args.build_dataset_num_proc,
850	        )
851	    print_with_rank(f"Dataset prepared with {len(eagle3_dataset)} samples.")
852	
853	    vocab_mapping_path = _generate_shared_vocab_mapping(
854	        eagle3_dataset,
855	        output_path=args.output_path,
856	        target_vocab_size=target_vocab_size,
857	        draft_vocab_size=draft_vocab_size,
858	    )
859	    if dist.get_rank() == 0:
860	        print(f"Vocabulary mapping ready at {vocab_mapping_path}")
861	
862	    # Create DP-sharded dataloader
863	    data_loader = prepare_dp_dataloaders(
864	        dataset=eagle3_dataset,
865	        batch_size=args.batch_size,
866	        num_workers=args.num_workers,
867	        shuffle=False,
868	        process_group=get_dp_group(),
869	    )
870	
871	    print_with_rank(
872	        f"DataLoader created for DP Rank {dist.get_rank(get_dp_group())}. "
873	        f"Number of batches: {len(data_loader)}"
874	    )
875	
876	    # Calculate starting index and sample count for current DP rank
877	    total = len(eagle3_dataset)
878	    dp_rank = dist.get_rank(get_dp_group())
879	    dp_size = dist.get_world_size(get_dp_group())
880	
881	    # Calculate samples per DP rank (handle non-divisible case)
882	    samples_per_dp = total // dp_size
883	    remainder = total % dp_size
884	
885	    # Earlier ranks handle one extra sample if there's a remainder
886	    if dp_rank < remainder:
887	        samples_per_dp += 1
888	        start_idx = dp_rank * samples_per_dp
889	    else:
890	        start_idx = dp_rank * samples_per_dp + remainder
891	
892	    print_with_rank(
893	        f"DP Rank {dp_rank} will process {samples_per_dp} samples, "
894	        f"starting from index {start_idx}"
895	    )
896	
897	    # Generate hidden states
898	    try:
899	        # Pass configurable arguments from args if needed
900	        with HiddenStatesGenerator(
901	            target_model,
902	            capture_layout=capture_plan.layout,
903	            num_io_threads=args.num_io_threads,
904	            io_queue_size=args.io_queue_size,
905	            file_group_size=args.file_group_size,
906	            compress=args.compress,
907	            compression_level=args.compression_level,
908	            # Other params like io_queue_size can also be added to argparse
909	        ) as hidden_states_generator:
910	
911	            # Generate hidden states
912	            hidden_states_generator.generate(
913	                data_loader,
914	                output_path=args.output_path,
915	                start_idx=start_idx,
916	                samples_per_dp=samples_per_dp,
917	            )
918	
919	    finally:
920	        # The finally block ensures destroy_distributed is always called
921	        print_with_rank("All hidden states generated or job finished.")
922	        destroy_distributed()
923	
924	
925	if __name__ == "__main__":
926	    main()

file.content:
"""Generate offline draft-training features with local SGLang capture.

The local target exists only for this preprocessing command. Online training
consumes features from an external server and never loads a target model in the
trainer process. Precomputing target features removes the target model's memory
and latency cost from the later offline training run. ``--strategy`` selects
the algorithm-owned capture layers and persisted tensor schema.
The hidden states are saved in ${output_path} and the vocabulary mapping is saved
in ${output_path}/vocab_mapping/vocab_mapping.pt
which is used for the offline training.

Usage:
torchrun --nproc_per_node=2 \
    scripts/prepare_hidden_states.py \
    --target-model-path Qwen/Qwen3-8B \
    --data-path ./cache/dataset/sharegpt_train.jsonl \
    --output-path ./cache/hidden_states/sharegpt_qwen3-8b \
    --chat-template qwen \
    --max-length 4096 \
    --tp-size 1 \
    --batch-size 32 \
    --num-samples 10000 \
    --strategy dspark \
    --draft-model-config configs/qwen3-8b-dspark.json


For pre-formatted data (with chat template already applied), add --is-preformatted:
torchrun --nproc_per_node=2 \
    scripts/prepare_hidden_states.py \
    --target-model-path Qwen/Qwen3-8B \
    --data-path ./cache/dataset/sharegpt_train.jsonl \
    --output-path ./cache/hidden_states/sharegpt_qwen3-8b \
    --chat-template qwen \
    --max-length 4096 \
    --tp-size 1 \
    --batch-size 32 \
    --num-samples 10000 \
    --strategy dspark \
    --draft-model-config configs/qwen3-8b-dspark.json \
    --is-performatted
"""

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class OfflineCapturePlan:
    """Resolved algorithm-owned feature schema for one preparation run."""

    strategy: str
    draft_config: object
    capture_method: str
    capture_layers: tuple[int, ...]
    layout: OfflineCaptureLayout


def parse_args():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()

    # model-related arguments
    model_group = parser.add_argument_group("model")
    model_group.add_argument("--target-model-path", type=str, required=True)
    model_group.add_argument(
        "--strategy",
        type=str,
        default="eagle3",
        help="Offline draft strategy (default: eagle3)",
    )
    model_group.add_argument(
        "--draft-model-config",
        type=str,
        default=None,
        help=(
            "Draft config used to resolve capture layers; required by strategies "
            "without target-derived defaults"
        ),
    )
    model_group.add_argument(
        "--trust-remote-code",
        action="store_true",
        help="Trust remote code when loading models",
    )
    data_group = parser.add_argument_group("data")
    data_group.add_argument("--data-path", type=str, required=True)
    data_group.add_argument("--max-length", type=int, default=2048)
    data_group.add_argument("--chat-template", type=str, default="llama3")
    data_group.add_argument(
        "--is-preformatted",
        action="store_true",
        help="Whether the input data is preformatted text with the chat template already applied to the conversation messages.",
    )
    data_group.add_argument("--num-samples", type=int, default=None)
    data_group.add_argument("--build-dataset-num-proc", type=int, default=8)

    inference_group = parser.add_argument_group("inference")
    inference_group.add_argument("--tp-size", type=int, default=1)
    inference_group.add_argument("--batch-size", type=int, default=32)

    others_group = parser.add_argument_group("others")
    others_group.add_argument("--cache-dir", type=str, default="./cache")
    others_group.add_argument("--output-path", type=str, default=None)
    others_group.add_argument(
        "--dist-timeout",
        type=int,
        default=2000,
        help="Timeout for collective communication in minutes, default to 2000 so that it does not go timeout",
    )
    others_group.add_argument(
        "--num-io-threads",
        type=int,
        default=None,
        help="Number of threads for async I/O operations (default: all of CPU cores).",
    )
    others_group.add_argument(
        "--num-workers", type=int, default=4, help="Number of workers for DataLoader"
    )
    others_group.add_argument(
        "--io-queue-size",
        type=int,
        default=50,
        help="Max number of pending I/O futures.",
    )
    others_group.add_argument(
        "--file-group-size",
        type=int,
        default=2000,
        help="Number of files per subdirectory.",
    )
    others_group.add_argument(
        "--compress",
        action="store_true",
        help="Compress hidden state files on disk (gzip).",
    )
    others_group.add_argument(
        "--compression-level",
        type=int,
        default=6,
        help="Gzip compression level (1-9).",
    )

    sglang_group = parser.add_argument_group("sglang")
    sglang_group.add_argument(
        "--sglang-attention-backend",
        default="flashinfer",
        help="Attention backend used by the offline SGLang capture",
    )
    sglang_group.add_argument("--sglang-mem-fraction-static", type=float, default=0.4)
    sglang_group.add_argument("--sglang-context-length", type=int, default=None)
    sglang_group.add_argument("--sglang-enable-nccl-nvls", action="store_true")
    sglang_group.add_argument("--sglang-enable-symm-mem", action="store_true")
    sglang_group.add_argument("--sglang-enable-torch-compile", action="store_true")
    sglang_group.add_argument("--sglang-enable-dp-attention", action="store_true")
    sglang_group.add_argument("--sglang-enable-dp-lm-head", action="store_true")
    sglang_group.add_argument("--sglang-ep-size", type=int, default=1)
    return parser.parse_args()


def _resolve_draft_vocab_size(source: str) -> int:
    """Load ``draft_vocab_size`` from one existing local JSON file."""

    expanded = Path(source).expanduser()
    if not expanded.is_file():
        raise FileNotFoundError(
            "--draft-model-config must point to an existing local JSON file: "
            f"{source}"
        )
    if expanded.suffix.lower() != ".json":
        raise ValueError(
            f"--draft-model-config must point to a local .json file: {source}"
        )
    try:
        with expanded.open(encoding="utf-8") as stream:
            payload = json.load(stream)
    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
        raise ValueError(f"invalid draft config JSON {expanded}: {exc}") from exc
    if not isinstance(payload, dict):
        raise ValueError(f"draft config JSON {expanded} must contain an object")
    value = payload.get("draft_vocab_size", payload.get("vocab_size"))

    if isinstance(value, bool) or not isinstance(value, int) or value <= 0:
        raise ValueError(
            f"draft model config {source!r} must define a positive "
            f"draft_vocab_size (or vocab_size fallback), got {value!r}"
        )
    return value


def _generate_shared_vocab_mapping(
    dataset,
    *,
    output_path: str,
    target_vocab_size: int,
    draft_vocab_size: int,
) -> str:
    """Generate one mapping on global rank 0 and propagate failures to peers."""

    mapping_dir = os.path.join(output_path, "vocab_mapping")
    mapping_path = os.path.join(mapping_dir, "vocab_mapping.pt")
    result = [None]
    if dist.get_rank() == 0:
        temporary_path = None
        try:
            temporary_key = f".vocab_mapping.{uuid.uuid4().hex}"
            temporary_path = generate_vocab_mapping_file(
                dataset=dataset,
                target_vocab_size=target_vocab_size,
                draft_vocab_size=draft_vocab_size,
                cache_dir=mapping_dir,
                cache_key=temporary_key,
            )
            mapping = torch.load(
                temporary_path,
                map_location="cpu",
                weights_only=True,
            )
            d2t = mapping.get("d2t")
            t2d = mapping.get("t2d")
            if not isinstance(d2t, torch.Tensor) or tuple(d2t.shape) != (
                draft_vocab_size,
            ):
                raise ValueError(
                    f"{temporary_path} has invalid d2t shape; expected "
                    f"({draft_vocab_size},), got {getattr(d2t, 'shape', None)}"
                )
            if not isinstance(t2d, torch.Tensor) or tuple(t2d.shape) != (
                target_vocab_size,
            ):
                raise ValueError(
                    f"{temporary_path} has invalid t2d shape; expected "
                    f"({target_vocab_size},), got {getattr(t2d, 'shape', None)}"
                )
            os.replace(temporary_path, mapping_path)
            temporary_path = None
            result[0] = {"path": mapping_path}
        except BaseException as exc:
            result[0] = {
                "error": f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}",
            }
        finally:
            if temporary_path is not None:
                try:
                    os.remove(temporary_path)
                except OSError:
                    pass

    dist.broadcast_object_list(result, src=0)
    if result[0] is None or "error" in result[0]:
        reason = "rank 0 did not publish a result"
        if result[0] is not None:
            reason = result[0]["error"]
        raise RuntimeError(f"failed to generate vocabulary mapping: {reason}")
    if result[0]["path"] != mapping_path:
        raise RuntimeError(
            "vocabulary mapping generator returned an unexpected path: "
            f"{result[0]['path']} != {mapping_path}"
        )
    return mapping_path


def _sglang_kwargs(args: argparse.Namespace) -> Dict[str, object]:
    return {
        "attention_backend": args.sglang_attention_backend,
        "mem_fraction_static": args.sglang_mem_fraction_static,
        "context_length": args.sglang_context_length,
        "enable_nccl_nvls": args.sglang_enable_nccl_nvls,
        "enable_symm_mem": args.sglang_enable_symm_mem,
        "enable_torch_compile": args.sglang_enable_torch_compile,
        "enable_dp_attention": args.sglang_enable_dp_attention,
        "enable_dp_lm_head": args.sglang_enable_dp_lm_head,
        "ep_size": args.sglang_ep_size,
        "max_running_requests": args.batch_size,
        "max_total_tokens": args.batch_size * args.max_length,
    }


def resolve_offline_capture_plan(
    args: argparse.Namespace,
    target_config: AutoConfig,
) -> OfflineCapturePlan:
    """Resolve capture layers and output keys through the algorithm registry."""

    strategy = getattr(args, "strategy", "eagle3")
    model = {
        "target_model_path": args.target_model_path,
        "draft_model_config": getattr(args, "draft_model_config", None),
        "trust_remote_code": args.trust_remote_code,
        "cache_dir": getattr(args, "cache_dir", None),
    }
    cfg = Config(
        model=model,
        data={
            "hidden_states_path": args.output_path or "__offline_capture__",
            "max_length": args.max_length,
        },
        training={"strategy": strategy},
    )
    resolved = resolve_offline_capture(cfg, target_config=target_config)
    return OfflineCapturePlan(
        strategy=resolved.run.algorithm.name,
        draft_config=resolved.draft_config,
        capture_method=resolved.capture_method,
        capture_layers=resolved.capture_layers,
        layout=resolved.layout,
    )


def build_target_model(
    args: argparse.Namespace,
    model_config: AutoConfig,
    capture_layers: List[int],
    capture_method: str = "eagle3",
) -> OfflineSGLangCapture:
    """Build the local target used only by this preprocessing command."""
    target_model = load_offline_capture(
        args.target_model_path,
        torch_dtype=(
            model_config.dtype
            if hasattr(model_config, "dtype")
            else model_config.torch_dtype
        ),
        trust_remote_code=args.trust_remote_code,
        **_sglang_kwargs(args),
    )
    target_model.set_capture_layers(
        capture_layers,
        capture_method=capture_method,
    )
    return target_model


class HiddenStatesGenerator:
    """
    This is a generator for creating and saving the hidden states based on the target model.
    It includes the following features:
        1. Fixes a potential deadlock in TP > 1 scenarios when a batch is skipped.
        2. Implements a context manager (`with` statement) for robust resource handling.
        3. Makes internal settings (like queue sizes, group sizes) configurable.
        4. Centralizes resource cleanup logic.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        target_model,
        capture_layout: Optional[OfflineCaptureLayout] = None,
        num_io_threads: int = 4,
        io_queue_size: int = 50,
        file_group_size: int = 2000,
        compress: bool = False,
        compression_level: int = 6,
    ):
        """
        Args:
            target_model: The model for inference.
            capture_layout: Algorithm-owned mapping from captured states to files.
            num_io_threads: Number of threads for async I/O.
            io_queue_size: Max number of pending I/O futures before cleanup.
            file_group_size: Number of files per subdirectory.
        """
        self.model = target_model
        self.capture_layout = capture_layout or OfflineCaptureLayout(
            capture_method="eagle3",
            aux_feature="aux_hidden_state",
            last_hidden_feature="hidden_state",
            passthrough=(
                ("input_ids", "input_ids"),
                ("loss_mask", "loss_mask"),
            ),
        )

        # --- Configurable parameters ---
        self.num_io_threads = num_io_threads
        self.io_queue_size = io_queue_size
        self.file_group_size = file_group_size
        self.compress = compress
        self.compression_level = compression_level
        self.file_extension = ".ckpt.gz" if self.compress else ".ckpt"

        # progress bar should only shown on TP rank = 0
        self.show_progress = dist.get_rank(get_tp_group()) == 0

        # --- REFACTOR: Thread pool is now managed by __enter__ and __exit__ ---
        self.io_executor = None
        self.pending_futures = []

    def __enter__(self):
        """Initializes resources when entering a 'with' block."""
        if is_tp_rank_0():
            self.io_executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=self.num_io_threads)
        self.pending_futures = []
        return self

    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb):
        """Cleans up resources when exiting a 'with' block."""
        if is_tp_rank_0() and self.io_executor is not None:
            if self.show_progress:
                print("\nWaiting for all async I/O operations to complete...")
            self._wait_all_saves()
            self.io_executor.shutdown(wait=True)
            self.io_executor = None  # Reset for safety

        # Final barrier to ensure all processes exit generate() cleanly
        dist.barrier()

    def _save_tensor_sync(
        self,
        record: Mapping[str, torch.Tensor],
        output_file: str,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Save a feature record synchronously, skipping records containing NaNs.

        Args:
            record: The algorithm-specific feature record to save.
            output_file (str): The path to the output file.
        """
        persisted = dict(record)
        for feature_name, tensor in persisted.items():
            if not torch.is_tensor(tensor):
                raise TypeError(
                    f"offline feature {feature_name!r} must be a tensor, got "
                    f"{type(tensor).__name__}"
                )
            if (torch.is_floating_point(tensor) or torch.is_complex(tensor)) and bool(
                torch.isnan(tensor).any()
            ):
                print(
                    f"Warning: NaN found in {feature_name} for {output_file}. "
                    "Skipping save."
                )
                return

        if self.compress:
            with gzip.open(
                output_file, "wb", compresslevel=self.compression_level
            ) as f:
                torch.save(persisted, f)
        else:
            torch.save(persisted, output_file)

    def _save_tensor_async(
        self,
        record: Mapping[str, torch.Tensor],
        output_file: str,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Submit a job to the io_executor to save the data point asynchronously.

        Args:
            record: The algorithm-specific feature record to save.
            output_file (str): The path to the output file.
        """
        assert is_tp_rank_0(), "Only tp_rank=0 should call _save_tensor_async"
        # If the queue of pending save operations is full, we must wait.
        if len(self.pending_futures) >= self.io_queue_size:
            # First, try to clear any futures that have already finished without waiting.
            self.pending_futures = [f for f in self.pending_futures if not f.done()]
            # If the queue is *still* full, it means all I/O threads are busy and we have
            # a backlog. We must now block the main generation loop and wait for the
            # oldest I/O operation to complete before proceeding.
            if len(self.pending_futures) >= self.io_queue_size:
                self.pending_futures.pop(0).result()

        future = self.io_executor.submit(self._save_tensor_sync, record, output_file)
        self.pending_futures.append(future)

    def _wait_all_saves(self):
        """
        This method is to ensure that all submitted jobs are completed.
        """
        if is_tp_rank_0() and self.pending_futures:
            for future in tqdm(
                self.pending_futures,
                desc="Finalizing Writes",
                disable=not self.show_progress,
            ):
                future.result()  # Wait and raise exception if any
            self.pending_futures.clear()

    def _prepare_output_dirs(
        self, output_path: str, start_idx: int, total_samples: int
    ) -> None:
        """
        The dataset is organized into groups of files, each group has a folder which contains the files for this group. For example, if the
        file_group_size is 2000, the 0-1999 samples will be saved in the folder "rows_0-2000", the 2000-3999 samples will be saved in the folder "rows_2000-4000", etc.

        Args:
            output_path (str): The path to the output directory.
            start_idx (int): The starting index of the samples to save.
            total_samples (int): The total number of samples to save.

        Returns:
            None
        """
        if not is_tp_rank_0() or total_samples == 0:
            return
        start_group = (start_idx // self.file_group_size) * self.file_group_size
        end_sample_idx = start_idx + total_samples - 1
        end_group = (end_sample_idx // self.file_group_size) * self.file_group_size
        for group_start_idx in range(start_group, end_group + 1, self.file_group_size):
            grouped_subdir = (
                f"rows_{group_start_idx}-{group_start_idx + self.file_group_size}"
            )
            output_dir = os.path.join(output_path, grouped_subdir)
            os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    def _check_existing_files_batch(
        self, output_path: str, global_indices: List[int]
    ) -> List[bool]:
        """
        A helper function to check if the files for the given global indices exist.

        Args:
            output_path (str): The path to the output directory.
            global_indices (List[int]): The global indices of the samples to check.

        Returns:
            List[bool]: A list of booleans indicating if the files for the given global indices exist.
        """
        if not is_tp_rank_0():
            return [False] * len(global_indices)

        def check_single_file(idx):
            if os.path.exists(self._get_file_path(output_path, idx)):
                return True
            uncompressed_ckpt = self._get_file_path(output_path, idx, extension=".ckpt")
            compressed_ckpt = self._get_file_path(
                output_path, idx, extension=".ckpt.gz"
            )
            return os.path.exists(uncompressed_ckpt) or os.path.exists(compressed_ckpt)

        # Parallel file existence check
        with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=self.num_io_threads) as executor:
            exists = list(executor.map(check_single_file, global_indices))
        return exists

    def _get_file_path(
        self, output_path: str, idx: int, extension: Optional[str] = None
    ) -> str:
        """
        A helper function to get the standard file path for the data point with the given index.

        Args:
            output_path (str): The path to the output directory.
            idx (int): The global index of the data point.

        Returns:
            str: The file path for the data point.
        """
        ext = self.file_extension if extension is None else extension
        group_idx = (idx // self.file_group_size) * self.file_group_size
        grouped_subdir = f"rows_{group_idx}-{group_idx + self.file_group_size}"
        return os.path.join(output_path, grouped_subdir, f"data_{idx}{ext}")

    @torch.no_grad()
    def generate(
        self,
        data_loader: torch.utils.data.DataLoader,
        output_path: str,
        start_idx: int = 0,
        samples_per_dp: int = 0,
    ):
        """
        This version prioritizes minimal CPU RAM usage above all else, even at the cost of performance.
        - It processes samples one-by-one within the tp_rank_0 process.
        - It avoids batching GPU-to-CPU transfers.
        - It ensures only one sample's data is in RAM for I/O at any given time.
        """
        self._prepare_output_dirs(output_path, start_idx, samples_per_dp)

        tp_group = get_tp_group()
        tp_group_ranks = dist.get_process_group_ranks(tp_group)
        tp_rank_0_global = tp_group_ranks[0]
        global_idx = start_idx

        progress_bar = tqdm(
            data_loader,
            disable=(not self.show_progress),
            desc="Generating Hidden States",
            position=dist.get_rank(get_dp_group()),
            leave=True,
        )

        total_skipped, total_processed = 0, 0

        for batch_idx, batch in enumerate(progress_bar):
            batch_size = batch["input_ids"].size(0)
            current_batch_indices = list(range(global_idx, global_idx + batch_size))

            # # Step 1: Synchronize valid indices across TP group
            # we check which files already exist and sync this info across TP ranks
            # if exists, we will skip these samples
            if is_tp_rank_0():
                exists_list = self._check_existing_files_batch(
                    output_path, current_batch_indices
                )
                exists_tensor = torch.tensor(
                    exists_list, dtype=torch.bool, device="cuda"
                )
            else:
                exists_tensor = torch.tensor(
                    [False] * batch_size, dtype=torch.bool, device="cuda"
                )
            dist.broadcast(exists_tensor, src=tp_rank_0_global, group=tp_group)

            # Step 1: TP rank 0 checks which samples need processing
            valid_indices_in_batch = [
                i for i, exists in enumerate(exists_tensor) if not exists
            ]
            sample_global_indices = [
                current_batch_indices[i] for i in valid_indices_in_batch
            ]
            num_valid = len(valid_indices_in_batch)
            total_skipped += batch_size - num_valid

            # Step 2: Filter batch before moving to GPU to save memory
            global_idx += batch_size
            filtered_batch = {
                "input_ids": batch["input_ids"][valid_indices_in_batch],
                "attention_mask": batch["attention_mask"][valid_indices_in_batch],
                "loss_mask": batch["loss_mask"][valid_indices_in_batch],
            }
            del batch
            if num_valid == 0:
                # Data has already been generated, no sample processing, update progress bar.
                if self.show_progress:
                    progress_bar.set_postfix(
                        {
                            "processed": total_processed,
                            "skipped": total_skipped,
                            "pending_io": (
                                len(self.pending_futures) if is_tp_rank_0() else 0
                            ),
                        }
                    )
                continue

            filtered_batch_gpu = {
                k: v.cuda(non_blocking=True) for k, v in filtered_batch.items()
            }
            captured = self.model.capture(
                **filtered_batch_gpu,
            )
            aux_hidden_states_list = captured.hidden_states
            last_hidden_states_list = captured.last_hidden_states
            if aux_hidden_states_list is None:
                aux_hidden_states_list = [None] * num_valid
            if last_hidden_states_list is None:
                last_hidden_states_list = [None] * num_valid

            del filtered_batch_gpu

            if is_tp_rank_0():
                for i, (
                    current_global_idx,
                    aux_hidden_states,
                    last_hidden_states,
                ) in enumerate(
                    zip(
                        sample_global_indices,
                        aux_hidden_states_list,
                        last_hidden_states_list,
                    )
                ):

                    # Process ONE sample at a time to minimize CPU RAM footprint
                    # 1. Transfer only the required slice for one sample to CPU
                    aux_hidden_states = (
                        aux_hidden_states.cpu().clone().unsqueeze(0)
                        if aux_hidden_states is not None
                        else None
                    )
                    last_hidden_states = (
                        last_hidden_states.cpu().clone().unsqueeze(0)
                        if last_hidden_states is not None
                        else None
                    )
                    record = self.capture_layout.materialize(
                        {
                            "input_ids": filtered_batch["input_ids"][i].clone(),
                            "loss_mask": filtered_batch["loss_mask"][i].clone(),
                            "aux_hidden_states": aux_hidden_states,
                            "last_hidden_states": last_hidden_states,
                        }
                    )

                    # 3. Save asynchronously (the backpressure logic is still crucial)
                    output_file = self._get_file_path(output_path, current_global_idx)
                    self._save_tensor_async(record, output_file)

                    # 4. Immediately clean up the single-sample CPU tensors
                    del last_hidden_states, aux_hidden_states

                total_processed += len(sample_global_indices)

            # Clean up the large GPU and CPU batch data
            del aux_hidden_states_list, last_hidden_states_list, filtered_batch

            if batch_idx % 5 == 0:  # Make GC and cache clearing more frequent
                torch.cuda.empty_cache()
                gc.collect()

            if self.show_progress:
                progress_bar.set_postfix(
                    {
                        "processed": total_processed,
                        "skipped": total_skipped,
                        "pending_io": (
                            len(self.pending_futures) if is_tp_rank_0() else 0
                        ),
                    }
                )

        if self.show_progress:
            print(
                f"\nGeneration loop finished. Processed: {total_processed}, Skipped: {total_skipped}"
            )
        dist.barrier()


def main():
    args = parse_args()
    if args.num_io_threads is None:
        cpu_cores = os.cpu_count() or 1
        args.num_io_threads = max(1, cpu_cores)
    if args.output_path is None:
        args.output_path = os.path.join(
            Path(__file__).parent.parent, "cache", "hidden_states"
        )

    target_model_config = AutoConfig.from_pretrained(
        args.target_model_path,
        cache_dir=args.cache_dir,
        trust_remote_code=args.trust_remote_code,
    )
    capture_plan = resolve_offline_capture_plan(args, target_model_config)
    draft_vocab_size = _resolve_draft_vocab_size(args.draft_model_config)

    # Initialize distributed environment (TP + DP)
    init_distributed(timeout=args.dist_timeout, tp_size=args.tp_size)
    print_args_with_dots(args)
    if dist.get_rank() == 0:
        print("Resolved draft model config:")
        print(capture_plan.draft_config.to_json_string(use_diff=False))
    print_with_rank(
        f"Resolved {capture_plan.strategy} offline "
        f"capture method {capture_plan.capture_method!r}, layers: "
        f"{list(capture_plan.capture_layers)}"
    )

    # Build target model (with TP)
    target_model = build_target_model(
        args,
        target_model_config,
        capture_layers=list(capture_plan.capture_layers),
        capture_method=capture_plan.capture_method,
    )
    target_text_config = getattr(
        target_model_config, "text_config", target_model_config
    )
    target_vocab_size = int(target_text_config.vocab_size)
    if draft_vocab_size > target_vocab_size:
        raise ValueError(
            f"draft_vocab_size={draft_vocab_size} exceeds target "
            f"vocab_size={target_vocab_size}"
        )

    print_with_rank(
        f"DP Rank {dist.get_rank(get_dp_group())}, TP Rank {dist.get_rank(get_tp_group())}, "
        f"DP Size {dist.get_world_size(get_dp_group())}, TP Size {dist.get_world_size(get_tp_group())}"
    )

    # Load complete dataset
    assert os.path.exists(
        args.data_path
    ), f"Dataset path {args.data_path} does not exist"

    with rank_0_priority():
        print_with_rank("Loading/building dataset cache...")
        dataset = Dataset.from_generator(
            generator=safe_conversations_generator,
            gen_kwargs={"file_path": args.data_path},
            cache_dir=os.path.join(
                os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))),
                "cache",
                "hf_dataset",
            ),
            num_proc=min(args.build_dataset_num_proc, 32),
        )
    if args.num_samples is not None:
        dataset = dataset.select(range(args.num_samples))
    # Tokenizer and cache key
    tokenizer = load_tokenizer(
        args.target_model_path, trust_remote_code=args.trust_remote_code
    )
    cache_params_string = f"{args.data_path}-{args.max_length}-{args.chat_template}-{args.target_model_path}-{args.num_samples}-{args.is_preformatted}"
    cache_key = hashlib.md5(cache_params_string.encode()).hexdigest()

    # Preprocess on complete, un-sharded dataset
    with rank_0_priority():
        print_with_rank("Main process is building the dataset cache...")
        eagle3_dataset = build_eagle3_dataset(
            dataset=dataset,
            tokenizer=tokenizer,
            chat_template=args.chat_template,
            max_length=args.max_length,
            cache_dir=os.path.join(args.cache_dir, "processed_dataset"),
            cache_key=cache_key,
            is_preformatted=args.is_preformatted,
            num_proc=args.build_dataset_num_proc,
        )
    print_with_rank(f"Dataset prepared with {len(eagle3_dataset)} samples.")

    vocab_mapping_path = _generate_shared_vocab_mapping(
        eagle3_dataset,
        output_path=args.output_path,
        target_vocab_size=target_vocab_size,
        draft_vocab_size=draft_vocab_size,
    )
    if dist.get_rank() == 0:
        print(f"Vocabulary mapping ready at {vocab_mapping_path}")

    # Create DP-sharded dataloader
    data_loader = prepare_dp_dataloaders(
        dataset=eagle3_dataset,
        batch_size=args.batch_size,
        num_workers=args.num_workers,
        shuffle=False,
        process_group=get_dp_group(),
    )

    print_with_rank(
        f"DataLoader created for DP Rank {dist.get_rank(get_dp_group())}. "
        f"Number of batches: {len(data_loader)}"
    )

    # Calculate starting index and sample count for current DP rank
    total = len(eagle3_dataset)
    dp_rank = dist.get_rank(get_dp_group())
    dp_size = dist.get_world_size(get_dp_group())

    # Calculate samples per DP rank (handle non-divisible case)
    samples_per_dp = total // dp_size
    remainder = total % dp_size

    # Earlier ranks handle one extra sample if there's a remainder
    if dp_rank < remainder:
        samples_per_dp += 1
        start_idx = dp_rank * samples_per_dp
    else:
        start_idx = dp_rank * samples_per_dp + remainder

    print_with_rank(
        f"DP Rank {dp_rank} will process {samples_per_dp} samples, "
        f"starting from index {start_idx}"
    )

    # Generate hidden states
    try:
        # Pass configurable arguments from args if needed
        with HiddenStatesGenerator(
            target_model,
            capture_layout=capture_plan.layout,
            num_io_threads=args.num_io_threads,
            io_queue_size=args.io_queue_size,
            file_group_size=args.file_group_size,
            compress=args.compress,
            compression_level=args.compression_level,
            # Other params like io_queue_size can also be added to argparse
        ) as hidden_states_generator:

            # Generate hidden states
            hidden_states_generator.generate(
                data_loader,
                output_path=args.output_path,
                start_idx=start_idx,
                samples_per_dp=samples_per_dp,
            )

    finally:
        # The finally block ensures destroy_distributed is always called
        print_with_rank("All hidden states generated or job finished.")
        destroy_distributed()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

10	
11	from __future__ import annotations
12	
13	from array import array
14	from typing import List, Optional
15	
16	import torch
17	import torch.distributed as dist
18	from sglang.srt.configs.model_config import ModelConfig
19	from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req, ScheduleBatch
20	from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler_components.dp_attn import prepare_mlp_sync_batch_raw
21	from sglang.srt.mem_cache.cache_init_params import CacheInitParams
22	from sglang.srt.mem_cache.radix_cache import RadixCache
23	from sglang.srt.model_executor.forward_batch_info import CaptureHiddenMode, ForwardBatch
24	from sglang.srt.sampling.sampling_params import SamplingParams
25	from sglang.srt.server_args import ServerArgs
26	from sglang.srt.speculative.spec_info import SpeculativeAlgorithm
27	from sglang.srt.utils import require_mlp_sync, require_mlp_tp_gather
28	
29	from specforge.distributed import get_tp_group
30	
31	from .model_runner import SGLangRunner
32	from .utils import wrap_offline_eagle3_logits_processors
33	
34	
35	class OfflineSGLangCaptureBackend:
36	    """Frozen local target used only to materialize offline features."""
37	
38	    def __init__(self, model_runner: SGLangRunner) -> None:
39	        self.model_runner = model_runner
40	
41	    @classmethod
42	    def build(
43	        cls,
44	        pretrained_model_name_or_path: str,
45	        *,
46	        torch_dtype: Optional[torch.dtype] = None,
47	        trust_remote_code: bool = False,
48	        **kwargs,
49	    ) -> "OfflineSGLangCaptureBackend":
50	        tp_size = dist.get_world_size(get_tp_group())
51	        server_args = ServerArgs(
52	            model_path=pretrained_model_name_or_path,
53	            trust_remote_code=trust_remote_code,
54	            dtype=torch_dtype if torch_dtype is not None else "auto",
55	            enable_return_hidden_states=True,
56	            disable_cuda_graph=True,
57	            chunked_prefill_size=-1,
58	            tp_size=tp_size,
59	            pp_size=1,
60	            **kwargs,
61	        )
62	
63	        tp_rank = dist.get_rank(get_tp_group())
64	        moe_ep_rank = tp_rank // (server_args.tp_size // server_args.ep_size)
65	        model_config = ModelConfig.from_server_args(server_args)
66	        model_runner = SGLangRunner(
67	            model_config=model_config,
68	            mem_fraction_static=server_args.mem_fraction_static,
69	            gpu_id=torch.cuda.current_device(),
70	            tp_rank=tp_rank,
71	            tp_size=server_args.tp_size,
72	            moe_ep_rank=moe_ep_rank,
73	            moe_ep_size=server_args.ep_size,
74	            pp_rank=0,
75	            pp_size=1,
76	            server_args=server_args,
77	            nccl_port=None,
78	            is_draft_worker=False,
79	        )
80	        model_runner.alloc_memory_pool()
81	        model_runner.init_attention_backends()
82	        model_runner.init_cuda_graphs()
83	        wrap_offline_eagle3_logits_processors(model_runner.model)
84	        return cls(model_runner)
85	
86	    def set_eagle3_capture_layers(self, layer_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None) -> None:
87	        self.model_runner.model.set_eagle3_layers_to_capture(layer_ids)
88	
89	    def set_capture_layers(
90	        self,
91	        layer_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
92	        *,
93	        capture_method: str,
94	    ) -> None:
95	        """Set auxiliary layers through the strategy's SGLang capture API."""
96	
97	        setter_name = {
98	            "eagle3": "set_eagle3_layers_to_capture",
99	            "dflash": "set_dflash_layers_to_capture",
100	        }.get(capture_method)
101	        if setter_name is None:
102	            raise ValueError(
103	                "offline SGLang capture method must be 'eagle3' or 'dflash', "
104	                f"got {capture_method!r}"
105	            )
106	        setter = getattr(self.model_runner.model, setter_name, None)
107	        if not callable(setter):
108	            raise RuntimeError(
109	                f"target model does not expose SGLang capture hook {setter_name!r}"
110	            )
111	        setter(layer_ids)
112	
113	    def _maybe_prepare_mlp_sync_batch(self, batch: ScheduleBatch) -> None:
114	        if require_mlp_sync(self.model_runner.server_args):
115	            prepare_mlp_sync_batch_raw(
116	                batch,
117	                dp_size=self.model_runner.server_args.dp_size,
118	                attn_tp_size=1,
119	                attn_cp_size=getattr(self.model_runner.server_args, "attn_cp_size", 1),
120	                tp_group=self.model_runner.tp_group,
121	                get_idle_batch=None,
122	                disable_cuda_graph=self.model_runner.server_args.disable_cuda_graph,
123	                require_mlp_tp_gather=require_mlp_tp_gather(
124	                    self.model_runner.server_args
125	                ),
126	                disable_overlap_schedule=self.model_runner.server_args.disable_overlap_schedule,
127	                offload_tags=set(),
128	            )
129	
130	    @torch.no_grad()
131	    def _forward_extend(self, reqs: list[Req]):
132	        cache_params = CacheInitParams(
133	            disable=False,
134	            req_to_token_pool=self.model_runner.req_to_token_pool,
135	            token_to_kv_pool_allocator=self.model_runner.token_to_kv_pool_allocator,
136	            page_size=self.model_runner.server_args.page_size,
137	        )
138	        batch = ScheduleBatch.init_new(
139	            reqs=reqs,
140	            req_to_token_pool=self.model_runner.req_to_token_pool,
141	            token_to_kv_pool_allocator=self.model_runner.token_to_kv_pool_allocator,
142	            tree_cache=RadixCache(cache_params),
143	            model_config=self.model_runner.model_config,
144	            enable_overlap=False,
145	            spec_algorithm=SpeculativeAlgorithm.NONE,
146	        )
147	        batch.prepare_for_extend()
148	        self._maybe_prepare_mlp_sync_batch(batch)
149	        if getattr(batch, "prefill_input_ids_cpu", None) is not None:
150	            batch.input_ids = batch.prefill_input_ids_cpu.to(
151	                batch.device, non_blocking=True
152	            )
153	            batch.prefill_input_ids_cpu = None
154	        batch.capture_hidden_mode = CaptureHiddenMode.FULL
155	        forward_batch = ForwardBatch.init_new(batch, self.model_runner)
156	        forward_batch.capture_hidden_mode = CaptureHiddenMode.FULL
157	        output = self.model_runner.forward(forward_batch)
158	        return output.logits_output if hasattr(output, "logits_output") else output
159	
160	    def _clear_pools(self) -> None:
161	        self.model_runner.req_to_token_pool.clear()
162	        self.model_runner.token_to_kv_pool_allocator.clear()
163	
164	    @torch.no_grad()
165	    def capture_eagle3(
166	        self,
167	        *,
168	        input_ids: torch.Tensor,
169	        attention_mask: torch.Tensor,
170	        loss_mask: torch.Tensor,
171	    ):
172	        """Capture per-request auxiliary and final hidden states without logits."""
173	
174	        sampling_params = SamplingParams(temperature=0, max_new_tokens=1, top_k=1)
175	        reqs: list[Req] = []
176	        data = []
177	        input_rows = torch.split(input_ids, 1, dim=0)
178	        attention_rows = torch.split(attention_mask, 1, dim=0)
179	        loss_rows = torch.split(loss_mask, 1, dim=0)
180	
181	        for idx, (input_row, attention_row, loss_row) in enumerate(
182	            zip(input_rows, attention_rows, loss_rows)
183	        ):
184	            req = Req(
185	                rid=str(idx),
186	                origin_input_text="",
187	                origin_input_ids=input_row.view(-1).tolist(),
188	                sampling_params=sampling_params,
189	            )
190	            req.full_untruncated_fill_ids = array("q", req.origin_input_ids)
191	            req.fill_len = len(req.full_untruncated_fill_ids)
192	            req.extend_input_len = req.fill_len - len(req.prefix_indices)
193	            req.logprob_start_len = len(req.origin_input_ids) - 1
194	            reqs.append(req)
195	            data.append((input_row, attention_row, loss_row))
196	
197	        input_lens = [len(req.origin_input_ids) for req in reqs]
198	        try:
199	            output = self._forward_extend(reqs)
200	            aux_hidden_states = getattr(output, "aux_hidden_states", None)
201	            last_hidden_states = getattr(output, "last_hidden_states", None)
202	            if aux_hidden_states is None or last_hidden_states is None:
203	                raise RuntimeError(
204	                    "SGLang did not return the hidden states required for "
205	                    "offline feature preparation"
206	                )
207	            aux_rows = torch.split(aux_hidden_states, input_lens, dim=0)
208	            last_rows = torch.split(last_hidden_states, input_lens, dim=0)
209	        finally:
210	            self._clear_pools()
211	
212	        return data, aux_rows, last_rows
213	
214	    def capture(
215	        self,
216	        *,
217	        input_ids: torch.Tensor,
218	        attention_mask: torch.Tensor,
219	        loss_mask: torch.Tensor,
220	    ):
221	        """Capture generic auxiliary and final target states."""
222	
223	        return self.capture_eagle3(
224	            input_ids=input_ids,
225	            attention_mask=attention_mask,
226	            loss_mask=loss_mask,
227	        )
228	
229	
230	__all__ = ["OfflineSGLangCaptureBackend"]
